title: Freezing the Notebook: An Interventional Invariant of Capability Formation Across Substrates
description: One gesture — freezing the slow variable a system writes and rereads (the 'notebook') — collapses the capability it carries, and, applied during training, prevents the capability from ever forming. Tested with pre-registered kill criteria across substrates (toy physics cascade, purpose-built in-context learners, pretrained transformers from four families), over seven capability types, crossing the implicit/explicit divide. Ships an interventional metrology of the notebook's dimension, its laws and their public corrections, a pedagogical companion ('A Walk with Emmy Noether'), and a tiered reproduction harness: the core result reruns on a laptop in minutes and in CI on every commit.
